steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage sequences three dyeing steps with "first" and "next." The blank introduces the third and final step. The needed transition must signal the last item in an ordered sequence, not contrast, cause-effect, or correction.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Finally,: Signals the last step in an ordered sequence — fits perfectly after "first" and "next." ✓
  • Choice B — However,: Signals contrast; removing cloth from a dye bath does not contradict or oppose simmering it. ✗
  • Choice C — Therefore,: Signals cause-and-effect; the transition needed is ordinal (this is the final step), not causal. ✗
  • Choice D — Actually,: Signals a correction or surprising reversal of an expectation; no expectation is being corrected here. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
